source.title: A ventral hippocampus-basolateral amygdala circuit regulates the unconditioned stimulus retrieval-induced reconsolidation of remote fear memory

confidence_rationale: Provides chemogenetic circuit-level evidence that the vCA1-BLA pathway specifically mediates reconsolidation triggered by unconditioned stimulus (US) retrieval — i.e., by harm-PE rather than CS retrieval. Inhibiting vCA1-BLA after US retrieval disrupts remote fear memory with long-term efficacy and resistance to reinstatement. This supports MECH-074d's claim that BLA (here receiving vHPC input) gates hippocampal memory updates specifically when harm-PE is high. The bidirectionality (activating vCA1-BLA blocks propranolol effect) strengthens the circuit claim.
failure_signatures: If the effect is specific to ventral HPC (motivational/affective axis) and does not generalise to dorsal HPC (spatial/planning axis, E3 viability map substrate), MECH-074d's remap_signal on the planning map is not supported.
If the circuit effect requires CS retrieval (not US/harm) to be engaged, the harm-PE trigger architecture is wrong.

mapping.source_claim_statement: The vCA1 → BLA circuit specifically supports US retrieval-induced (harm-PE-driven) reconsolidation of remote fear memory; inhibiting this circuit after US retrieval produces long-lasting memory disruption; activating it blocks propranolol's reconsolidation-blocking effect.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-074d requires that BLAAnalog emits remap_signal specifically when harm-PE (z_harm_a - E2_harm_a_pred) is high. Guo et al. show the hippocampus-BLA circuit is specifically activated by US retrieval (harm event reactivation), supporting a PE-triggered pathway rather than a generic retrieval trigger. The vCA1 → BLA direction is the opposite of MECH-074d's BLA → HPC remap_signal, but both directions of the bidirectional circuit are needed for the full update loop.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The paper focuses on vCA1 → BLA (hippocampus feeding BLA) whereas MECH-074d's remap_signal is BLA → hippocampus (BLA triggering hippocampal remap). Both directions of the circuit are empirically supported in this paper (chemogenetic manipulation of vCA1-BLA affects both encoding and reconsolidation), but the directional claim in MECH-074d requires the BLA → HPC direction specifically.
mapping.source_context: Rat contextual fear conditioning, recent vs remote memory, US and CS retrieval conditions, chemogenetics (DREADDs), fiber photometry, pharmacology.

confidence_components.notes: J Adv Res 2025, full paper. Chemogenetic circuit-level causal design is strong. Mapping caveat: vCA1 not dorsal HPC; directionality is bidirectional, not just BLA->HPC.
